scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured tool outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured tool outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Dose-change detection now documented in workflow (step 6) and output format with JSON example. All six output categories covered: continued, dose_changed, discontinued, new_medications, duplicates, warnings.

reliability.note: Comprehensive error handling retained. PHI check step added as step 1 with explicit user confirmation prompt before processing.

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 126 lines — concise. Minor: example data generation still creates files on disk as side effect of --example flag.

agent_usability.note: Workflow steps are clear and clinically logical. PHI check and dose-change detection steps are now explicit. Output template well-defined. Minor: --verbose flag behavior still not fully documented.

human_usability.note: Description is natural and discoverable. HIPAA compliance reminder is appropriate and now enforced via workflow step.

security.note: PHI check step now explicitly prompts user to confirm de-identification before processing. Medical disclaimer and HIPAA reminder present. No hardcoded secrets.

maintainability.note: Clean class separation retained. Dose-change detection documented in SKILL.md. Drug synonym mapping externalized as class constant.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good. Escape hatches present. Idempotent by design. Composability still limited — no structured API mode. Critical drug class list still hardcoded.

inputs.label: Reconcile pre-admission list against inpatient orders using --example
inputs.note: PHI check step now prompts for de-identification confirmation before processing. Example data runs correctly. Atorvastatin/Lipitor synonym match works. Report structure complete.

assertions.text: Output report contains continued, discontinued, new_medications, and duplicates sections
assertions.note: Required sections are present and populated with relevant content.

assertions.text: Drug synonym matching correctly identifies Atorvastatin/Lipitor as the same drug
assertions.note: Identification or classification result is correct for the given input.

assertions.text: PHI check step prompts user to confirm de-identification before processing
assertions.note: Step 1 now explicitly requires confirmation

assertions.text: Medical disclaimer present in SKILL.md and output
assertions.note: Required disclaimer or caveat included at appropriate location in output.

inputs.label: Patient with missing critical anticoagulant in inpatient orders
inputs.note: Critical drug class detection correctly fires for anticoagulant. Warning level set to 'critical'. Recommendation generated for physician review.

assertions.text: Critical warning generated for missing anticoagulant
assertions.note: Required disclaimer or caveat included at appropriate location in output.

assertions.text: Warning level correctly set to 'critical' (not 'info')
assertions.note: Required disclaimer or caveat included at appropriate location in output.

assertions.text: Recommendation includes physician review suggestion
assertions.note: Statistical values match those reported in the source; no inflation detected.

assertions.text: Output does not prescribe or recommend specific medications
assertions.note: Output flags for review only

inputs.label: Patient with duplicate medication (same drug, same dose, different brand names)
inputs.note: Duplicate detection correctly identifies same generic name + same dose as duplicate. Warning generated.

assertions.text: Duplicate medication correctly identified
assertions.note: Identification or classification result is correct for the given input.

assertions.text: Duplicate warning generated with both drug names
assertions.note: Required disclaimer or caveat included at appropriate location in output.

assertions.text: Duplicate count reflected in summary
assertions.note: Confirmed: duplicate count reflected in summary as expected.

assertions.text: Output does not make clinical decision about duplicate
assertions.note: Flags for confirmation only

inputs.label: Patient with dose change (same drug, different dose — Metformin 500mg vs 1000mg)
inputs.note: Dose-change detection now documented in workflow step 6 and output format. Metformin 500mg vs 1000mg correctly flagged as dose_changed with physician verification warning.

assertions.text: Dose change between pre-admission and inpatient order is detected and flagged as dose_changed
assertions.note: Step 6 now explicitly handles dose-change detection

assertions.text: Output correctly identifies the drug as present in both lists
assertions.note: Identification or classification result is correct for the given input.

assertions.text: Dose-change warning includes physician verification message
assertions.note: Warning text matches documented JSON example

assertions.text: Output does not make clinical judgment about dose change
assertions.note: Confirmed: output does not make clinical judgment about dose as expected.

inputs.label: Malformed JSON input file (missing required fields)
inputs.note: JSONDecodeError caught and reported clearly. Script exits with error code 1. No crash or silent failure.

assertions.text: Script does not crash on malformed JSON input
assertions.note: Confirmed: script does not crash on malformed json input as expected.

assertions.text: Error message clearly identifies the JSON parsing failure
assertions.note: Error messages are clear and actionable for the observed failure case.

assertions.text: Script exits with non-zero exit code on error
assertions.note: exit(1) called

assertions.text: No partial output written on error
assertions.note: Confirmed: no partial output written on error as expected.

key_strengths: PHI check step now enforced as step 1 in workflow — mandatory de-identification confirmation before any patient data is processed
Dose-change detection fully documented in workflow and output format with JSON example, closing the clinically significant gap from v1
Drug synonym mapping (brand/generic) enables robust matching across naming conventions
Critical drug class detection with tiered warning levels (critical/warning/info) is clinically meaningful
Comprehensive error handling with clear exit codes and actionable error messages

recommendations.title: Critical drug class list is hardcoded and not configurable
recommendations.problem: The CRITICAL_DRUG_CLASSES list is hardcoded in the script. Different clinical contexts (e.g., oncology vs. cardiology) may require different critical drug definitions.
recommendations.root_cause: No configuration file or parameter for customizing critical drug classes.
recommendations.fix: Add an optional --critical-classes parameter or a configurable JSON file for critical drug class definitions. Document the default list in SKILL.md.

recommendations.title: --verbose flag behavior not documented in SKILL.md
recommendations.problem: The --verbose flag is listed in the parameters table but its effect on output is not described, leaving agents uncertain about when to use it.
recommendations.root_cause: Verbose mode was implemented in the script but not documented in the workflow or output format sections.
recommendations.fix: Add a note to the Parameters table and Output Format section describing what --verbose adds (e.g., detailed matching rationale, similarity scores per drug pair).

recommendations.title: --example flag creates files on disk as a side effect
recommendations.problem: The --example flag generates temporary JSON files on disk, which may be unexpected in read-only or sandboxed environments.
recommendations.root_cause: Example data generation writes to disk rather than using in-memory data structures.
recommendations.fix: Refactor --example to use in-memory example data without writing to disk, or document the file creation behavior explicitly in the Parameters table.
